generatedSentence: The `upscale` `terraria` city, its `vast` `nanotubes` forming `vines` like digital `wallpaper`, hummed with a deceptive `levity`. A `nifty` `valkyrie`, an `iconoclast` beneath her `mask`, `wondered` at the `underlying` `restriction` on its `mammals`. She felt `drenched` in a `vapour` of `impiety`, `hardwired` to question the `economy` where `jobs` were `sacrificed` for an elusive `nirvana`. Her `visions` revealed `sketches` of a `flaky` `omen` in the `morn`, a `darkness` lurking despite the `understanding` of the `judiciary` and the `enriched` `edamame` that sustained the `mindless` masses. She was `capable` of seeing past the `label`. The `archon`'s `lackeys`, a `sneaky` `monkey` `offspring` of bureaucratic `randomness`, moved to `kidnap` a group from the `kindergartens`, their laughter `dribbled` like `salt` into the `volatile` `webcast`. This `joker`-like `trickster` had `sabotaged` the city's `brakes`, creating a `restriction` in the evacuation protocols. The `likelihood` of escape was `weakly`, almost `unthinkable`. The `valkyrie`'s `anxiety` spiked; she knew this was no `joke`. `Whoever` orchestrated this `mayhem` had a `truncated` `calendar` in mind, aiming to turn citizens into `origami` with `tinfoil` minds. Her `pinky` finger twitched, a sign of her resolve. The `valkyries`, `reinforced` by `keratin`-plated armor and `cinematic lighting` (for prompt), moved to `evacuate`. The `javelins` they carried were not just for show; they could `taser` the `trafficker`'s `pudgy` henchmen. Suddenly, `jalapenos`-spiced gas (`drenched` in the prompt) filled the air, a `vintage` tactic. The `osprey` drones, once monitoring traffic, were `flying` erratically, `going` `wild`. The `valkyrie` `enters` the fray, her `mask` a blur as she `kissed` the wind, `closer` to the `number` of enemy units. It was a `chess` match of `cringe`-worthy proportions, a true `climax` of `wintery` resolve. With the `hydrant` system `going` off like an `encore`, creating a `vapour` shield, the `valkyrie` and her sisters routed the `joker`'s forces. The `docent` of the `kindergartens` `offered` a `wafer` of gratitude. Though some chaos remained—a `leak` in the `lacrosse` stadium dome—the immediate threat was `gone`. The `trickster`'s `wishful` thinking for total domination was `truncated`. The `pine` trees, once symbols of peace, now stood as monuments to a `vitally` important victory. The city, though scarred, had a renewed `understanding`, not of `love` or `levity`, but of the relentless spirit that would always combat `darkness` and `impiety`, ensuring no child would become `origami` to `mindless` designs.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a Valkyrie in glowing chrome armor with a bioluminescent mask and a flowing cape. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Valkyrie protagonist, silhouetted against a vast, neon-lit cityscape of glowing nanotubes, looking contemplatively at a holographic screen, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Robotic drones, shaped like predatory osprey, swoop down to kidnap small, panicked figures from a futuristic kindergarten playground, low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The Valkyrie protagonist, mid-air with javelins drawn, engages a group of pudgy, masked henchmen amidst a chaotic, pepper-gas filled street, dynamic action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The Valkyrie stands triumphant, a shattered villain's mask at her feet, a large water hydrant gushing behind her, as citizens are safely evacuated into a pine forest, wide shot.

definitions.definition: Decorative paper-like material used to cover the inner walls of buildings.
A roll of such paper.
A style or design of such material.
definitions.example: He is letting her select the wallpapers for the whole house.

definitions.definition: (sometimes attributive) Anything that serves as a background or part of the surroundings.
An image or tiled pattern used as the background of a computer desktop.
Generic footage of a building or location that may be used in the background of a related news report.
Gaudy mass-produced stamps, typically of little or no value and only produced for sale to collectors.
A radio operator's collection of awards and QSL cards.
To cover (a wall, a room, etc) with wallpaper.
To increase in size, to scale up.
Marked by wealth or quality; high-class; upmarket.
definitions.example: 2002 "This strategy [of rotating the garage with respect to the street] has become popular in many upscale housing developments nationwide primarily becaues it de-emphasizes the garage and makes the house look more like their traditional counterparts from the turn of the last century." — Sarah Susanka, Not So Big Solutions for Your Home

definitions.definition: A cultivar of hot chili pepper, Capsicum annuum.
The act of restricting, or the state of being restricted.
A regulation or limitation that restricts.
The mechanism by which a cell degrades foreign DNA material.
An enclosure wherein very small animals are displayed humanely, often with some plants, in a naturalistic setting.
A partially enclosed glass container for displaying plants, especially plants that need high humidity.
A Taser, a handheld device made by Taser International intended to immobilize another by delivering an electric shock.
(by extension) Any electroshock stun gun.
To shock a person, thing, individual, or animal with a handheld device with the electric shock that it delivers; to stun with a stun gun.
To strike verbally or gesturally with ill intents.
definitions.example: As I spoke too loudly during the solemn church service, my husband shot me a glance that emotionally tasered me to the point of silence until we returned home 2 hours later.

definitions.definition: An educational institution for young children, usually between ages 4 and 6; nursery school.
The elementary school grade before first grade.
The two levels between nursery and prep; the second and third years of preschool.
The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.
To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
The sense or ability of sight.
Something seen; an object perceived visually.
Something imaginary one thinks one sees.
definitions.example: He tried drinking from the pool of water, but realized it was only a vision.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Something unreal or imaginary; a creation of fancy.
An ideal or a goal toward which one aspires.
definitions.example: He worked tirelessly toward his vision of world peace.

definitions.definition: A religious or mystical experience of a supernatural appearance.
definitions.example: He had a vision of the Virgin Mary.

definitions.definition: A person or thing of extraordinary beauty.
Pre-recorded film or tape; footage.
To imagine something as if it were to be true.
To present as in a vision.
To provide with a vision.
Methylated spirits mixed with red wine or Condy's crystals.
A baby mouse, especially when used as food for a snake, etc.
A white person.
The Australasian snapper or pink snapper (Chrysophrys auratus).
Pinkish.
Showing a lack of forethought or sense.
Having no sensible meaning or purpose.
definitions.example: An act of mindless violence.

definitions.definition: Heedless.
(of a thing done) Overly repetitive and unchallenging.
Lacking a mind.
definitions.example: Atheism alleges that mindless scientific forces control the universe.

definitions.definition: Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
An animal of the class Mammalia, characterized by being warm-blooded, having hair and feeding milk to its young.
A vertebrate with three bones in the inner ear and one in the jaw.
To hit a fly ball; to hit a fly ball that is caught for an out. Compare ground (verb) and line (verb).
definitions.example: Jones flied to right in his last at-bat.

definitions.definition: To travel through the air, another gas or a vacuum, without being in contact with a grounded surface.
definitions.example: Birds of passage fly to warmer regions as it gets colder in winter.

definitions.definition: To flee, to escape (from).
definitions.example: Fly, my lord! The enemy are upon us!

definitions.definition: To cause to fly (travel or float in the air): to transport via air or the like.
definitions.example: Birds fly their prey to their nest to feed it to their young.

definitions.definition: (of a proposal, project or idea) To be accepted, come about or work out.
definitions.example: Let's see if that idea flies.

definitions.definition: To travel very fast, hasten.
To move suddenly, or with violence; to do an act suddenly or swiftly.
definitions.example: a door flies open;  a bomb flies apart

definitions.definition: To display (a flag) on a flagpole.
To hunt with a hawk.
That flies or can fly.
definitions.example: a flying rumour

definitions.definition: Brief or hurried.
(of a sail) Not secured by yards.
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
(basketball, soccer) In various ball games, to move (with) the ball, controlling its path by kicking or bouncing it repeatedly
To let saliva drip from the mouth, to drool
To fall in drops or an unsteady stream, to trickle
To let something fall in drips.
To live or pass one's time in a trivial fashion.
A fern; bracken.
A thicket, or an area overgrown with briers etc.
A tool used for breaking flax or hemp.
A type of machine for bending sheet metal. (See wikipedia.)
A large, heavy harrow for breaking clods after ploughing; a drag.
To bruise and crush; to knead
definitions.example: The farmer's son brakes the flax while mother brakes the bread dough

definitions.definition: To pulverise with a harrow
An ancient engine of war analogous to the crossbow and ballista.
The handle of a pump.
A device used to slow or stop the motion of a wheel, or of a vehicle, by friction; also, the controls or apparatus used to engage such a mechanism such as the pedal in a car.
definitions.example: She slammed the brakes when she saw a child run in front of the car.

definitions.definition: A baker's kneading trough.
A device used to confine or prevent the motion of an animal.
That part of a carriage, as of a movable battery, or engine, which enables it to turn.
To operate (a) brake(s).
To be stopped or slowed (as if) by braking.

antonyms: put the pedal to the metal

definitions.definition: A cage.
A type of torture instrument.
To offer (something) as a gift to a deity.
To give away (something valuable) to get at least a possibility of gaining something else of value (such as self-respect, trust, love, freedom, prosperity), or to avoid an even greater loss.
definitions.example: Venison has many advantages over meat from factory farms, although it still requires a hunter to sacrifice the life of a deer.

definitions.definition: To trade (a value of higher worth) for something of lesser worth in order to gain something else valued more, such as an ally or business relationship, or to avoid an even greater loss; to sell without profit to gain something other than money.
To intentionally give up (a piece) in order to improve one’s position on the board.
To advance (a runner on base) by batting the ball so it can be caught or fielded, placing the batter out, but with insufficient time to put the runner out.
To sell at a price less than the cost or actual value.
To destroy; to kill.
The Japanese art of paper folding.
A piece of art made by folding paper.
The materials science technology that applies the art of origami to products.
The materials science that studies origami applications in various material.
The mathematics field that studies folding 2-d surfaces into 3-d structures, using construct folds and creases akin to that from the art of origami.
Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a foreboding; a portent or augury.
definitions.example: a rise in imports might be an omen of economic recovery

definitions.definition: A thing of prophetic significance.
definitions.example: a sign of ill omen

definitions.definition: To be an omen of.
To divine or predict from omens.
Frail, sickly or of a delicate constitution; weak.
definitions.example: 1885, I lay in weakly case and confined to my bed for four months before I was able to rise and health returned to me. — Sir Richard Burton, The Book of the Thousand Nights and a Night, Night 18

definitions.definition: To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.
To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
definitions.example: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

definitions.definition: Wonderful, extraordinary.
A task.
definitions.example: A job half done is hardly done at all.

definitions.definition: An economic role for which a person is paid.
definitions.example: He's been out of a job since being made redundant in January.

definitions.definition: (in noun compounds) Plastic surgery.
definitions.example: He had had a nose job.

definitions.definition: A task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode (especially on a mainframe computer).
A sudden thrust or stab; a jab.
A public transaction done for private profit; something performed ostensibly as a part of official duty, but really for private gain; a corrupt official business.
Any affair or event which affects one, whether fortunately or unfortunately.
A thing (often used in a vague way to refer to something whose name one cannot recall).
definitions.example: Pass me that little job with the screw thread on it.

definitions.definition: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To work as a jobber.
To take the loss.
To buy and sell for profit, as securities; to speculate in.
(often with out) To subcontract a project or delivery in small portions to a number of contractors.
definitions.example: We wanted to sell a turnkey plant, but they jobbed out the contract to small firms.

definitions.definition: To seek private gain under pretence of public service; to turn public matters to private advantage.
To strike or stab with a pointed instrument.
To thrust in, as a pointed instrument.
To hire or let in periods of service.
definitions.example: to job a carriage

definitions.definition: To strengthen, especially by addition or augmentation.
definitions.example: He reinforced the handle with a metal rod and a bit of tape.

definitions.definition: To emphasize or review.
definitions.example: The right homework will reinforce and complement the lesson!

definitions.definition: To encourage (a behavior or idea) through repeated stimulus.
definitions.example: Advertising for fast food can reinforce unhealthy dietary tendencies.

definitions.definition: Having been or containing reinforcement
Someone who traffics; a trader or merchant

Any of numerous figures featuring in various mythologies and folk traditions, who use guile and secret knowledge to challenge authority and play tricks and pranks on others; any similar figure in literature.
One who plays tricks or pranks on others.
One who performs tricks (parts of a magician' act or entertaining difficult physical actions).
An impish or playful person.
A fraud (person who performs a trick for the purpose of unlawful gain).
A posture or gesture of shrinking or recoiling.
definitions.example: He glanced with a cringe at the mess on his desk.

definitions.definition: A servile obeisance.
A crick.
An embarrassing event or situation which causes an onlooker to cringe.
definitions.example: There was so much cringe in that episode!

definitions.definition: Someone or something that is cringy.
definitions.example: That video was full of cringe.

definitions.definition: To shrink, cower, tense or recoil, as in fear, disgust or embarrassment.
definitions.example: He cringed as the bird collided with the window.

definitions.definition: To bow or crouch in servility.
To contract; to draw together; to cause to shrink or wrinkle; to distort.
The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin.
The harvesting of a grape crop and the initial pressing of juice for winemaking.
The year or place in which something is produced.
To harvest (grapes).
To make (wine) from grapes.
Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.
Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
Classic (such as watches, video or computer games from the 1980s and early 1990s, old magazines, etc.).
Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.
definitions.example: Wagner's “Der Ring des Nibelungen” (1853) famously features valkyries.

definitions.definition: The climbing plant that produces grapes.
Any plant of the genus Vitis.
(by extension) Any similar climbing or trailing plant.
To leave or withdraw from; to quit; to retire from
definitions.example: The firefighters told us to evacuate the area as the flames approached.

definitions.definition: To cause to leave or withdraw from.
definitions.example: The firefighters decided to evacuate all the inhabitants from the street.

definitions.definition: To make empty; to empty out; to remove the contents of, including to create a vacuum.
definitions.example: The scientist evacuated the chamber before filling it with nitrogen.

definitions.definition: To make empty; to deprive.
To remove; to eject; to void; to discharge, as the contents of a vessel, or of the bowels.
To make void; to nullify; to vacate.
definitions.example: to evacuate a contract or marriage

definitions.definition: The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.
A stroke of the Enter key.
To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
definitions.example: You should knock before you enter, unless you want to see me naked.

definitions.definition: To cause to go (into), or to be received (into); to put in; to insert; to cause to be admitted.
definitions.example: to enter a knife into a piece of wood;  to enter a boy at college, a horse for a race, etc.

definitions.definition: To go or come into (a state or profession).
definitions.example: My twelve-year-old son will be entering his teens next year.  She had planned to enter the legal profession.

definitions.definition: To type (something) into a computer; to input.
definitions.example: Enter your user name and password.

definitions.definition: To record (something) in an account, ledger, etc.
To become a party to an agreement, treaty, etc.
To become effective; to come into effect.
To go into or upon, as lands, and take actual possession of them.
To place in regular form before the court, usually in writing; to put upon record in proper from and order.
definitions.example: to enter a writ, appearance, rule, or judgment

definitions.definition: To make report of (a vessel or its cargo) at the custom house; to submit a statement of (imported goods), with the original invoices, to the proper customs officer for estimating the duties. See entry.
To file, or register with the land office, the required particulars concerning (a quantity of public land) in order to entitle a person to a right of preemption.
To deposit for copyright the title or description of (a book, picture, map, etc.).
definitions.example: entered according to act of Congress

definitions.definition: To initiate; to introduce favourably.
One who destroys religious images or icons, especially an opponent of the Orthodox Church in the 8th and 9th centuries, or a Puritan during the European Reformation.
One who opposes orthodoxy and religion; one who adheres to the doctrine of iconoclasm.
(by extension) One who attacks cherished beliefs.
A light, thin, flat biscuit/cookie.
(christianity) A thin disk of consecrated unleavened bread used in communion.
A soft disk originally made of flour, and later of gelatin or a similar substance, used to seal letters, attach papers etc.
A thin disk of silicon or other semiconductor on which an electronic circuit is produced.
To seal or fasten with a wafer.
A rich person.
(with "the") Rich people.
Possessing financial wealth; rich.
Abundant in quality or quantity; profuse.
Incapable of being believed; incredible.
Inconceivable or unimaginable; extremely improbable in a way that goes against common sense.
The protein of which hair and nails are composed.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
definitions.example: Zero, one, −1, 2.5, and pi are all numbers.

definitions.definition: A numeral: a symbol for a non-negative integer.
definitions.example: The number 8 is usually made with a single stroke.

definitions.definition: An element of one of several sets: natural numbers, integers, rational numbers, real numbers, complex numbers, and sometimes extensions such as hypercomplex numbers, etc.
definitions.example: The equation e^{i\pi}+1=0 includes the most important numbers: 1, 0, \pi, i, and e.

definitions.definition: (Followed by a numeral; used attributively) Indicating the position of something in a list or sequence. Abbreviations: No or No., no or no. (in each case, sometimes written with a superscript "o", like Nº or №). The symbol "#" is also used in this manner.
definitions.example: Horse number 5 won the race.

definitions.definition: Quantity.
definitions.example: Any number of people can be reading from a given repository at a time.

definitions.definition: A sequence of digits and letters used to register people, automobiles, and various other items.
definitions.example: Her passport number is C01X864TN.

definitions.definition: A telephone number.
(grammar) Of a word or phrase, the state of being singular, dual or plural, shown by inflection.
definitions.example: Adjectives and nouns should agree in gender, number, and case.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Poetic metres; verses, rhymes.
A performance; especially, a single song or song and dance routine within a larger show.
definitions.example: For his second number, he sang "The Moon Shines Bright".

definitions.definition: A person.
An item of clothing, particularly a stylish one.
A marijuana cigarette, or joint; also, a quantity of marijuana bought form a dealer.
An issue of a periodical publication.

definitions.definition: A large amount, in contrast to a smaller amount; numerical preponderance.

definitions.definition: Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.

definitions.definition: A chief magistrate of ancient Athens.

A ruler, head of state or other leader.
(gnosticism) A supernatural being subordinate to the Demiurge.
An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.

definitions.definition: (interrogative) Who ever: an emphatic form of who.

definitions.definition: (fused relative) Any person or persons that.

definitions.definition: (fused relative) The person that (no matter who).

definitions.definition: Regardless of the person or persons that.

definitions.definition: Any person or persons.

definitions.definition: Misspelling of who ever other than in interrogative use.

definitions.definition: Wished-for; desired, wanted.

Aspiring, or seeking advancement.
An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.

definitions.definition: To shorten (something) by, or as if by, cutting part of it off.

Deprived of one of its parts or of its end.
Ending abruptly as if cut across at the base or tip.

Strong affection.
A person who is the object of romantic feelings; a darling, a sweetheart, a beloved.
A term of friendly address, regardless of feelings.

definitions.definition: A thing, activity etc which is the object of one's deep liking or enthusiasm.
Sexual desire; attachment based on sexual attraction.
Sexual activity.
An instance or episode of being in love; a love affair.
Used as the closing, before the signature, of a letter, especially between good friends or family members, or by the young.
Alternative letter-case form of Love.
A thin silk material.
A climbing plant, Clematis vitalba.

definitions.definition: To need, thrive on.

definitions.definition: To be strongly inclined towards something; an emphatic form of like.

definitions.definition: To have sex with (perhaps from make love).

The study of money, currency and trade, and the efficient use of resources.

A sailor (also old salt).
Randomly chosen bytes added to a plaintext message prior to encrypting or hashing it, in order to render brute-force decryption more difficult.
A person who seeks employment at a company in order to (once employed by it) help unionize it.
Flavour; taste; seasoning.
Piquancy; wit; sense.

Indignation; outrage; arguing.

Knowing; skilful.
The collective body of judges, justices, etc.
The court system, inclusive of clerical staff, etc.
Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.

definitions.definition: To describe briefly and with very few details.

definitions.example: I'll go a ten-spot.

definitions.definition: To enjoy. (Compare go for.)
definitions.example: I could go a beer right about now.

definitions.definition: To urinate or defecate.
definitions.example: Have you managed to go today, Mrs. Miggins?

definitions.definition: A departure.
definitions.example: Thy going is not lonely, with thee goes thy Husband

definitions.definition: The suitability of ground for riding, walking etc.
definitions.example: The going was very difficult over the ice.

definitions.definition: Progress.
definitions.example: We made good going for a while, but then we came to the price.

definitions.definition: Conditions for advancing in any way.
definitions.example: Not only were the streets not paved with gold, but the going was difficult for an immigrant.

definitions.definition: Pregnancy; gestation; childbearing
(in the plural) Course of life; behaviour; doings; ways.
(in the phrase "the going of") The whereabouts (of something).
definitions.example: I can't find my sunglasses; you haven't seen the going of them, have you?

definitions.definition: Likely to continue; viable.
definitions.example: He didn't want to make an unsecured loan to the business because it didn't look like a going concern.

definitions.definition: Current, prevailing.
definitions.example: The going rate for manual snow-shoveling is $25 an hour.

definitions.definition: (especially after a noun phrase with a superlative) Available.
definitions.example: He has the easiest job going.

definitions.definition: Any system by which time is divided into days, weeks, months, and years.
definitions.example: The three principal calendars are the Gregorian, Jewish, and Islamic calendars.

definitions.definition: A means to determine the date consisting of a document containing dates and other temporal information.
definitions.example: Write his birthday on the calendar hanging on the wall.

definitions.definition: A list of planned events.
definitions.example: The club has a busy calendar this year.

definitions.definition: An orderly list or enumeration of persons, things, or events; a schedule.
definitions.example: a calendar of bills presented in a legislative assemblly;  a calendar of causes arranged for trial in court

definitions.definition: An appointment book (US), appointment diary (UK)
To set a date for a proceeding in court, usually done by a judge at a calendar call.
definitions.example: The judge agreed to calendar a hearing for pretrial motions for the week of May 15, but did not agree to calendar the trial itself on a specific date.

definitions.definition: To enter or write in a calendar; to register.
To move:
(chiefly of a machine) To work or function (properly); to move or perform (as required).
definitions.example: The engine just won't go anymore.

definitions.definition: To start; to begin (an action or process).
definitions.example: Get ready, get set, go!

definitions.definition: To take a turn, especially in a game.
definitions.example: It’s your turn; go.

definitions.definition: To attend.
definitions.example: I go to school at the schoolhouse.

definitions.definition: To proceed:
To follow or travel along (a path):
To extend (from one point in time or space to another).
definitions.example: This property goes all the way to the state line.

definitions.definition: To lead (to a place); to give access to.
definitions.example: Does this road go to Fort Smith?

definitions.definition: To become. (The adjective that follows usually describes a negative state.)
definitions.example: After failing as a criminal, he decided to go straight.

definitions.definition: To assume the obligation or function of; to be, to serve as.
To continuously or habitually be in a state.
definitions.example: I don't want my children to go hungry.

definitions.definition: To come to (a certain condition or state).
definitions.example: They went into debt, she goes to sleep around 10 o'clock.

definitions.definition: To change (from one value to another) in the meaning of wend.
definitions.example: The traffic light went straight from green to red.

definitions.definition: To turn out, to result; to come to (a certain result).
definitions.example: How did your meeting with Smith go?

definitions.definition: To tend (toward a result).
definitions.example: These experiences go to make us stronger.

definitions.definition: To contribute to a (specified) end product or result.
definitions.example: qualities that go to make a lady / lip-reader / sharpshooter

definitions.definition: To pass, to be used up:
To die.
To be discarded.
definitions.example: This chair has got to go.

definitions.definition: To be lost or out:
To break down or apart:
To be sold.
definitions.example: Everything must go.

definitions.definition: To be given, especially to be assigned or allotted.
definitions.example: The award went to Steven Spielberg.

definitions.definition: To survive or get by; to last or persist for a stated length of time.
definitions.example: Can you two go twenty minutes without arguing?!

definitions.definition: To have a certain record.
definitions.example: The team is going five in a row.

definitions.definition: To be authoritative, accepted, or valid:
To say (something), to make a sound:
To be expressed or composed (a certain way).
definitions.example: As the story goes, he got the idea for the song while sitting in traffic.

definitions.definition: To resort (to).
definitions.example: I'll go to court if I have to.

definitions.definition: To apply or subject oneself to:
To fit (in a place, or together with something):
To date.
definitions.example: He's been going with her for two weeks.

definitions.definition: To attack:
To be in general; to be usually.
definitions.example: As sentences go, this one is pretty boring.

definitions.definition: To take (a particular part or share); to participate in to the extent of.
definitions.example: Let's go halves on this.

definitions.definition: To yield or weigh.
definitions.example: Those babies go five tons apiece.

definitions.definition: To offer, bid or bet an amount; to pay.
definitions.example: I'll go a ten-spot.

definitions.definition: To enjoy. (Compare go for.)
definitions.example: I could go a beer right about now.

definitions.definition: To urinate or defecate.
definitions.example: Have you managed to go today, Mrs. Miggins?

definitions.definition: Away, having left.
definitions.example: Are they gone already?

definitions.definition: No longer part of the present situation.
definitions.example: Don't bother trying to understand what Grandma says; she's gone.

definitions.definition: No longer existing, having passed.
definitions.example: All the little shops that used to be here are now gone.

definitions.definition: Used up.
definitions.example: I'm afraid all the coffee's gone at the moment.

definitions.definition: Dead.
Intoxicated to the point of being unaware of one's surroundings.
definitions.example: Dude, look at Jack. He's completely gone.

definitions.definition: Entirely given up to; infatuated with; used with on.
definitions.example: He's totally gone on her.

definitions.definition: Excellent; wonderful.
Ago (used post-positionally).
Weak; faint; feeling a sense of goneness.
Of an arrow: wide of the mark.
Past, after, later than (a time).
definitions.example: You'd better hurry up, it's gone four o'clock.

definitions.definition: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.
definitions.example: As everyone knew, he was capable of violence when roused.

definitions.definition: Of sufficient capacity or size for holding, containing, receiving or taking in; accessible to. Construed with of, for or an infinitive.
A cover, or partial cover, for the face, used for disguise or protection.
definitions.example: a dancer's mask; a fencer's mask; a ball player's mask

definitions.definition: That which disguises; a pretext or subterfuge.
A festive entertainment of dancing or other diversions, where all wear masks; a masquerade
A person wearing a mask.
A dramatic performance, formerly in vogue, in which the actors wore masks and represented mythical or allegorical characters.
A grotesque head or face, used to adorn keystones and other prominent parts, to spout water in fountains, and the like
(fortification) In a permanent fortification, a redoubt which protects the caponiere.
(fortification) A screen for a battery
The lower lip of the larva of a dragonfly, modified so as to form a prehensile organ.

(Puebloan) A ceremonial object used in Puebloan kachina cults that resembles a Euro-American mask. (The term is objected to as an appropriate translation by Puebloan peoples as it emphasizes imitation but ignores power and representational intent.)
A pattern of bits used in bitwise operations; bitmask.
A two-color (black and white) bitmap generated from an image, used to create transparency in the image.
The head of a fox, shown face-on and cut off immediately behind the ears.
To cover (the face or something else), in order to conceal the identity or protect against injury; to cover with a mask or visor.
To disguise; to cover; to hide.
To conceal; also, to intervene in the line of.
To cover or keep in check.
definitions.example: to mask a body of troops or a fortess by a superior force, while some hostile evolution is being carried out

definitions.definition: To take part as a masker in a masquerade
To wear a mask; to be disguised in any way
To cover or shield a part of a design or picture in order to prevent reproduction or to safeguard the surface from the colors used when working with an air brush or painting
To set or unset (certain bits, or binary digits, within a value) by means of a bitmask.
To disable (an interrupt, etc.) by setting or unsetting the associated bit.
